questions.prompt: You have an OCR service + an LLM + custom JSON parsing for processing receipts. Costs are high and failures are common. What's the modern Claude pattern?

choices.text: Add another LLM pass for verification.

Replace the whole stack with one Claude call: pass the receipt image + define a `save_receipt` tool with the desired schema + force tool_choice. Claude reads the image natively (OCR implicit) and returns typed schema-valid data. One service, one call, schema-enforced output.

questions.explanation: Modern multimodal models like Claude collapse OCR + LLM + parser pipelines into a single call. Vision input + tool-call structured output is the canonical pattern for receipt scanning, invoice extraction, ID parsing, and many similar workflows.

questions.prompt: Why downscale large phone photos before sending to Claude?

choices.text: Image tokens scale with resolution. A 4000×3000 photo costs many times more tokens than a 1280×960 version. For most reading tasks, the smaller version is just as accurate. Downscale before sending; pay 90% less for the same quality.

questions.explanation: Token count is the cost driver and it tracks image resolution. Most reading tasks (receipts, screenshots, document scans) don't need full phone-camera resolution. Downscale to ~1000–1500px on the long edge before sending. Token cost drops, accuracy holds.
